title: townsendia parryi
intro: Townsendia parryi, also known as Parry's Townsendia, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the family Asteraceae and is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbalism. The plant's roots and leaves are used to make herbal preparations that are said to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Townsendia parryi can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
